data.title: As an operator, the large-commit review threshold is documented and configurable in the project configuration file
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Add a review.huge_diff_file_threshold entry to CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md and dso-config.example.conf, documenting the key's purpose, default value, valid range, and validation error behavior.

**Why**: Without documentation, operators cannot discover or safely configure the threshold. The example config ensures new project installations include the key with a correct default, preventing silent failures where the key is absent and an undocumented default is applied.

**Scope**:
- IN: CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md entry for review.huge_diff_file_threshold (purpose, default 20, valid range: positive integers only, validation error on 0 or negative), dso-config.example.conf entry with inline comment
- OUT: implementation of threshold validation (Story S1), runtime behavior documentation in CLAUDE.md (Story S7)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md includes an entry for review.huge_diff_file_threshold describing the key's purpose, default value (20), valid range (positive integers only), and the validation error behavior triggered by 0 or negative values
  ← Satisfies: "CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md and dso-config.example.conf include the new review.huge_diff_file_threshold key with default value (20) and valid range (positive integers only)"
- When this story is complete, dso-config.example.conf includes review.huge_diff_file_threshold=20 with an inline comment explaining the valid range and what the threshold controls

  ← Satisfies: "CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md and dso-config.example.conf include the new review.huge_diff_file_threshold key with default value (20) and valid range (positive integers only)"

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
